mechanism_narrative: A declining trend in FEDFUNDS reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present value of the S&P 500 and producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days, independent of any threshold condition.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window (Sahm-rule-style trigger) signals recession onset, causing investors to discount future earnings sharply and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of historical trigger episodes, SPX 60-day forward returns following the trigger are positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signal broad-based consumer demand weakness that is not captured by either series alone, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations for consumer-facing sectors are revised down.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline and HOUST MoM decline occur concurrently for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint decline episodes produce SPX 20-day forward returns that are not statistically different from unconditional baseline returns (p > 0.05), or single-series models outperform the joint signal in out-of-sample IC.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, a rising INDPRO signals capacity utilization pressure that reinforces inflation persistence fears, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; in an easing regime, the same rising INDPRO instead signals genuine recovery and produces positive returns, so the direction of the effect flips with the monetary regime.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Sign of the INDPRO-forward-return relationship does not flip between tightening and easing regimes as classified by FEDFUNDS trend, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 in the tightening regime specifically.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects ongoing labor income generation supporting consumption and corporate revenue, producing a modest but persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days, always active regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return following positive PAYEMS prints is not significantly greater than following negative prints (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above a persistence threshold implying central bank hawkishness, markets price in higher-for-longer rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the equity risk premium rises.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY inflation exceeds 4% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns during trigger episodes are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or fewer than 55% of trigger episodes show negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth signals durable, broad-based demand expansion that outlasts a single quarter's data noise, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days because earnings revisions across sectors compound rather than offset.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% and RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform the unconditional 60-day baseline return by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) across rolling 3-year windows, or GDPC1 data revisions materially reverse the growth classification more than 20% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime with FEDFUNDS elevated, a low and falling UNRATE signals persistent labor market tightness that reinforces fears of sustained wage-driven inflation, causing the central bank to stay hawkish and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days; in a low-rate regime the same low UNRATE instead supports risk-on sentiment.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with tight labor market
failure_condition: The negative return relationship does not hold specifically in tightening-regime subsamples (Rank IC < 0.03) even though it may hold unconditionally, or reverses sign when regime classification is applied.
